The whole reason of having a "student view" on My Classroom is so that teachers can display the seating plan on the board. Although it removes the photos and "EAL/SEN, etc" codes, it still has the points. This is absolutely not information that we would want other students to see - it is somewhat in breach of data rules.

 If we can't toggle the points off, staff are going to have to manually type up/edit the seating plans when they display them on the large screen for students to know where to sit. summary: How are you feeling included in the morning and afternoon register to track emotional wellbeing
comment:
Good morning,

 My name is Lara and I am the pastoral lead at Beechfield School.

 I have talked with Richard in the morning regarding how it could be beneficial to incorporate something like checking in how pupils feel when taking the register. At school we use zones of regulation - each zone blue, green, yellow, red represent a series of feelings. What I was thinking is: when teachers take the register they could also add the zone onto the register.

 Pupils, would be explained in advance and would only say the colour to the teacher.

 This could be beneficial for us to monitor how pupils feel not only on the day but during the week, month (identify potential patterns, check ins from the pastoral team...). It has to be something that teachers can do very quickly.

 It is just an idea, happy to discuss further.

 Thank you so much.

comment:
It takes too long to add attendance notes in Arbor - there are way too many different steps. We have to:

 1. go to Bulk Edit Marks >

 2. input the appropriate filters for date and group >

 3. find the student in the list >

 4. add a present mark from the drop down >

 5. add the attendance note >

 6. remove the attendance mark (i.e. change it back to a dash) by carefully navigating up the narrow little arrow.

 This is also the case when we have to remove or edit a note. We have a large number of students who leave lessons for instrumental tuition and various meetings with outside agencies, so it's quite a laborious task to make sure the registers are up to date so their teachers are aware.

 I'd like to suggest a solution - could it be something that's done through the students' calendar? It means we would only need to

 1.search for the student in the home page

 2. open their calendar for the week

 3. right click on the lesson where the attendance note needs adding, and have an option to add the note there.

 PLEASE TAKE ON BOARD THESE SUGGESTIONS AS ARBOR'S CURRENT PROCESS IS NOT EFFICIENT AT ALL.

 Many thanks
journeys: Taking Period-by-Period Registers

summary: Message recipient groups - ability to edit group for one message only
comment:
Please could you look into the possibility of allowing users to edit a group in the communications/messaging feature?

 Currently the options are to manually add a lits of students one-by-one, or select a whole predetermined group (e.g. class or year) without the ability to select (or remove) specific students from that group.

 It'd be really helpful if we could, for example, select a whole year group or class group but then be able to unselect a couple of students (in case of long-term absence or similar).

 The solution I have been given so far is to create a custom group with the students I would want to communicate with, send the message, then delete the group but this seems an overly-complicated solution for users. Our previous MIS messaging system had this feature where you could pick the whole group and then untick certain contacts/students from that group.

 Thank you so much for your consideration. If there's an easy way of doing this that I'm missing I am sorry!

1) Please can you look into how you can easily cancel a club session when students are assigned to the session? Our free clubs may be cancelled because of poor weather, a clash of events, staff illness etc, and it would be good to be able to cancel the session and it send an email out to parents/guardians so that they are aware. summary: Report of the number of students who attended each session and interval for a wraparound club
comment:
Hi, see communication below.

 _Hi Caroline,

 I've been able to take a look into this a bit further.

 Sadly, it's not currently possible to report on session intervals directly. The easiest way to see who is on a certain interval would be to navigate to the session overview for the day you want to check. From there, you will see a list of students who are signed up and there is a column that displays which session interval they have signed up for.

 For example, this is the session overview for today's session. I got to this page via School > Activities > Clubs > Years 2-6 ASC > Club Overview > Clicked into "Every Monday" under the Sessions section > On the slide-over clicked into "On 26th Jan". Alternatively you can navigate to the session via Students > Attendance > Registers > Daily Attendance > Years 2-6 ASC > On the slide-over click Session Overview button.

 When on the session interval, you can then filter the Session Interval column:

 And select to view a specific interval, for example, you can just tick 15:15 - 15:45 to simply bring up those on that session interval:

 Then you can see the number of students in that session interval by checking the number at the bottom of the table:

 Unfortunately, there's no way to check this in bulk or for multiple days. If you did want to see improvements to this, and the reporting options for clubs in general, then we would welcome feedback on this. To learn more about our roadmap and how to submit feedback, please take a look at our guidance here.

 It would be so helpful to be able to report easily on this.

 The data is there, surely we should be able to have a report with all the info.

 Thank you

 Caroline

role: Set detentions on an automatic, pre scheduled day
comment:
At my school we run a system where all detentions are capped at being booked for the next academic day.

 This means that students who are frequently set detentions do not get booked up for days ahead which we feel takes away the impact, meaning and outcome of the detention as students are often unaware why they have been set.

summary: Have the option to add dates, times and instances to reports
comment:
I use the Students - Attendance - Latecomer feature to create reports for our house teams to show when a student has the following:

 1. The student has been late to lessons with their name, reg, year and house

 2. Which lesson they were late for with a separate line for each lesson

 3. The date and time the student was late with a separate line for each instance

 4. How many minutes the student was late for each instance

 5. The mark the student received

 I tried to use the custom report writer to replicate this report, so I could schedule the report, rather than running this report; this is something I run at the same time, on the same day of the week, each week.

 Unfortunately, the custom report writer does not have this functionally; it will not show the individual instances of the lateness with date and time and lesson name. This is also the case for statutory roll call.

 Can these columns be added to the custom report writer?

 It would be useful for staff to identify these students so patterns of behaviour can be identified (is it the same lessons each week? Is it the same periods each week? Have they had a bad week or is this something consistent behaviour?

 This would reduce the amount of time I spend creating and adjusting these reports each week, allowing me to use more time in a more productive way. This is especially pertinent with the introduction of Arbor AI.

 Please can you inform me if this suggestion is being considered as the suggestions I have sent previously seem to go into the void. Can you let users know if their suggestions are being reviewed, are under consultation, if they are being actioned or will not be actioned and why, if the suggestion has been made before. Is there a place you can post idea so users know what is being suggested? A place where users can vote on possible future functions? This will create a better sense of community and make users feel a little they are being heard. Thank you

comment:
Please see ticket and response below. This is unworkable in reality in a secondary school.

 This was my ticket: Hi, is there a quicker way of completing level 1 approvals. Current a Head of Department has to go into each class twice - one for the WAG approval and one for the PFG. summary: Suggestion: Improving Group Detention Creation to Reduce Teacher Workload
role: KS4 Subject Lead Computer Science & Digital Lead of EdTech
comment:
Currently, when creating a detention for a group, teachers are required to manually check that every individual student does not already have a detention scheduled on that date. This process is not feasible in practice. It creates a significant administrative burden and adds unnecessary workload for already time-pressured staff.

 At present, if even one student in a class already has a detention, the date is removed from the available list entirely. This prevents teachers from assigning detentions to the rest of the group, even when the majority of students are available. As a result, teachers must either abandon the date or manually check each student, which involves excessive clicks and time.

 Other systems (for example, Edulink) handle this more effectively by:

 -- Assigning the detention automatically to students who are available

 -- Flagging or reporting any students with clashes

 -- Informing the teacher of conflicts without blocking the entire process

 A similar approach would significantly improve usability and efficiency. Teachers do not have the capacity to follow the current multi-step checking process, and the number of clicks required is disproportionate to the task. Streamlining this workflow would reduce workload, improve staff experience, and better reflect the realities of classroom practice.

comment:
We have a lot of students in our school on part-time timetables.

 For example now I'm updating a student to start at 12:30 (our P4), so coming out of all lessons in Guidance, P1, P2, Excellence and P3.

 The way it works in Arbor, you have to manually remove them from every individual lesson as an exclusion, which is a LOT of flicking through tabs, copy/pasting, changing dates, etc.

 For this student I'm having to manually exclude her from 50 timetable slots (5 lessons per day as above, over our 10 day timetable). This takes up a massive amount of time for what shouldn't really be that huge a task.

 What I would suggest is some kind of part-time timetable button where you can enter a start/end time and it automatically excludes them from all lessons before/after this time. For example, if I put 12:30 as her start time, it would auto-exclude her from all current lessons before that.

 I know lots of other schools also have a large amount of part-time timetables, and it is only becoming more common, so this would be a helpful feature for many Arbor schools.

Issue with students seeing a later finish time on their Arbor account than what they get because there is one student in the same room as them that gets ET so the time shown for all students is the latest finishing time which has confused many students at our school as they believe that they are entitled to ET when in fact they do not get it!

summary: Pre-filled marks in a student's planned absence aren't updated if the planned absence is updated after a register has been opened
comment:
Sometimes a planned absence is added but a student might unexpectedly come into school before the planned absence end time, in which case the planned absence end time will be updated, and a pre-filled mark may be changed within the planned absence. The register may have been opened accidentally by a teacher during the original planned absence timescale, or because a lesson has already started and the student has come in just after this.

 In these scenarios, the only way around this is to update the planned absence end time and pre-filled mark and then manually update the mark in marks by date too. This is long-winded, and there is a risk of incorrect marks being left on student attendance records if a member of staff forgets to update marks by date too. Ideally, if a planned absence and pre-filled mark is updated, this will update on the attendance records of a student even if the register has been opened already.

comment: There are a number of permissions where there are multiple smaller permissions lumped together. The prime example of this is the "General Admin:Administer" permission within the School category. While at face value this single permission takes on a number of high level admin tasks that should be heavily restricted, such as setting up the next school year or setting up the guardian portal. But at the same time there are a lot of very low level permissions that any normal school would look at passing off as low level admin, such as approving student profile record changes or creating meetings. With this being set up as it is, giving pastoral admin staff the low level admin tasks will also allow them to create and delete year set ups, have access to the full behavioural set up, turn on/off the guardian portal. These are a security weakness with even trying to give the lowest possible access to staff that they require will give multiple high level accesses that could be prevented with more granular permissions.

comment:
Dear Arbor Support Team,

 I am writing to formally raise several critical issues regarding the Clubs Module within the Arbor Management Information System. These technical shortcomings are currently causing significant administrative distress for our front-office staff and frustration for our parent body.

 We would like to bring the following three areas to your immediate attention:

 1. Lack of "Capacity Full" Notifications

 The system currently fails to provide a clear "Club Full" notification to parents once capacity is reached. This lack of transparency leads to a high volume of avoidable phone calls and emails from parents seeking clarification, placing an unnecessary manual burden on our administration team.

 2. Absence of Basket Expiry (Reservation Timer)

 We have identified a significant flaw in the booking workflow: parents are able to hold places in their "basket" indefinitely without completing the checkout or payment process.

 The Impact: These spaces are effectively "locked," preventing other parents from booking them, even if the initial user has no intention of following through.

 Requested Resolution: We require an automatic "release" function (a reservation timer) that returns the space to the public pool if payment is not confirmed within a set timeframe (e.g., 10‚Äì15 minutes).

 3. Ineffective Archiving on the Parent App

 The Arbor App continues to display inactive and historical clubs from previous terms and academic years. This lack of a clear "Active/Inactive" filter or automatic archiving function is causing widespread confusion. Parents are frequently attempting to view or join clubs that ended years ago because the interface is cluttered with legacy data.

 The current state of the module is not fit for purpose for a busy school environment. It is creating more work for our staff rather than streamlining it.

 Yours sincerely,

 Thorpe Hall Primary School Office
journeys: Parent and guardian messages and notes|Managing End-of-Day Dismissals and Clubs|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

comment:
Hi,

 This request is from one of our TAs

 "In a lot of the classes I'm in, there is a lot of students who are going for time outs and are not reporting to the Learning Hub or are abusing the system. As far as I can see we are not reporting on how much time students are spending out of lessons. We are concerend about puncutality and attendance but there is a culture emerging of students being present and on time but sit and wait for the middle half hour of the lesson to leave consitantly every lesson. The report would then reset every lesson.

 Jill mentioned on the inset about using the classroom layout function to allocate ATL's. Is there any way we can use this to tap students in and out? So in the middle of a lesson, student asks to leave the class room, you click on the students seat, and a quick reasons menu appears, toilet, time out, early pass, behaviour etc. This then collects on a report with a timer. So time out would then populate a report for whoever is on cover in the Learning Hub, they would see the name of that student and click to say arrived. A five minute timer would start and then that name would change colour to signal they need to leave. LH cover would then click to sent child out and the teacher in the classroom would then click the seating plan to signal they had returned to lesson. A report would then be generated and monitored to show just how much learning time has taken place out of the classroom and if needed reported to parents.

 Could the same interface then be used to call for senior cover or if we go back to toilet monitoring a toilet report created. There then is a report generated for medical evidence. Or if there is a gap of no one has picked up this child then its investigated as to where this student was.

 It could then even be that if a student asks to leave but does not have the relevant pass, that option does not appear for them, so permission to leave for that reason cant be given or the reasonable adjustment pops up, ie stand in the corridor for time out 2 mins but the data is recorded. "

 Cheers

summary: Behaviour setup so that students who have existing detentions are not given future detentions.
comment:
Hi,

 We have recently joined Arbor after a few years with Sims and prior to that a lengthy period of time with Bromcom.

 We believe that detentions should not "just" be used as a sanction but as a means for staff and students to have meaningful restorative conversations to reduce the repeat of such incidents.

 Even if a student has two separate incidents in a day, what is important is for them to have the short restorative conversations during the detention period (reflection period) with all members of staff involved.

 We do not want detentions to be set for students who already have detentions but behaviour points can still be awarded.

 Staff who intend to issue detentions should get a notification that the student already has a detention on the day and should therefore not be able to issue another detention for a future date.

 To summarise, we do not want staff to be able to set future detentions for a student who already has an upcoming detention. Please help.

comment:
We conducted a Lockdown drill and we needed a register from Arbor that has all students and all staff, this cannot be house, class or form registers as students could be anywhere. For example if they were in the toilet when the alert sounds they have been instructed to go to the nearest, classroom or office so it is imperative that the students are not grouped. I did contact yourselves and must admit I was very concerned that this was not something you had, as we cannot be the only school with this issue. The solutions offered involved setting up a report and printing it, but that would not work as how do we collate that when all classrooms are locked, which is why we cannot use the fire register.

 The register needs to show if the child is not in school and it needs to be online so that everyone can access it at the same time to mark that that student is safe. Without this there is a safeguarding issue as we would not know that all students were accounted for.

 In a lockdown scenario we cannot print anything and our office staff will move out of the main office for safety reasons so they and the rest of our employees need to be able to record which students they have with them as soon as possible.
journeys: Accessing Vital Pupil Context|Conducting Emergency Evacuation Registers|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Designing and Managing Seating Plans|Accessing Vital Pupil Context and SEND Passports|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

Currently the meal bookings cancels any meals for children that are absent - which is a great feature, however it also cancels meals for children that may be attending an appointment / visit in the morning but are returning back to school during the morning session and therefore still require their lunch.

 At the moment, office staff need to check that any students with 'M' marks (as an example) have booked a lunch, and if so need to re-add their lunch choice onto the booking once the register has been taken, as Arbor identifies them as absent.

 It would be really useful to either be able to add criteria to the 'absent meal provision' so that only children with certain codes have their meals cancelled (such as 'I' 'E') where it is known that children will be absent for the whole day and therefore the meal isn't required - Or to have a prompt that when you are recording a planned absence or general absence, there is a feature that asks if the child still requires their meal booking, and the member of staff can select either 'yes' or 'no'. Another option would be - If the time of the absence was set to finish before lunch, then Arbor doesn't cancel the meal option?

 An example of one I had today - A child went to visit their secondary school, and their absence was put in from 08:30am - 10:00am. They still required their hot meal at school, however as they had been marked as a 'V' their lunch was cancelled.. https://holy-rood-catholic-primary-school.uk.arbor.sc/?/student-ui/overview/id/1347/global-search-type/1/

 I'm not sure if something along these lines could be developed but it would be really useful for our school to provide accurate lunch numbers to the kitchen - especially as we are on a split site school and where transporting additional meals across sites isn't always easy.

 Thank you

 Kelly

summary: Planned absence codes should include present mark
comment:
I know this probably sounds daft, but we have many many reasons why students will have planned absence from a lesson but will be in school. The class teacher would therefore mark N.

 If this is REG or P4 at least, this will translate to N for Roll Call which will be incorrect.

 It would also trigger texts home alerting parents to children "unexpectedly not in school" who actually are in school all the time but at e.g. individual music instrument lessons, one off events like Maths challenges etc. As the vast majority of such pupils are present, it is better to have the present mark pre-set which can be overwritten by an N when needed, rather than an N pre-set which would need to be overwritten (quickly in the case of REG) when the pupil is indeed where they are expected to be.)

 This could be solved by simply including the present mark in the planned absence (as that is the only way to put a note explaining absence from lesson and also include a mark)

comment:
This formula calculates maximum possible attendance %age across the academic year:

 =ROUNDUP((((Term 1/100*38)+(Term 2/100*34)+(Term 3/100*30)+(Term 4/100*17)+(13)+(23)+(35))/190*100),1)

 It can be varied to show a particular Term (4):

 =ROUNDUP((((/100*38)+(/100*34)+(/100*30)+(/100*17)+(13))/132*100),1)

 The days passed and remaining days of the current term have to manually adjusted on the day the report runs to make it accurate. It would be great to have an Arbor report which adjusts the variables automatically on the day it runs.

comment:
I watched a webinar with Inclusive Attendance Ltd and they shared a useful idea for identifying the pupils at risk of PA based on their numbers of days absence. The first column contained names of pupils with 19+ days of absence (and therefore already locked into PA for the end of the year); the second column contained the names of children with between 18 and 15 days of absence (high risk for PA); the third column contained the names of children with between 14 and 10 days of absence (moderate risk for PA); the final column had the names of children with 5+ days of PA (high concern for PA). If the final column (5+ days) could include in brackets after each child's name, the number of spells of absence, that would be useful (particularly if this list could be organised in ascending or descending order). Even better, would be if clicking on each child in the final list could produce a pop-up box which details the reasons and dates for the child's absence - that way staff could assess the risk of a pattern developing more easily.

 If Arbor could create this table, that would be really useful! If it could also be used retrospectively for past academic years to check back and identify pupils who are likely to present a risk of PA in the future, then that would be great. Thank you!

role: Executive IT Manager / Invictus Education Trust
comment:
Our old bespoke/in-house VLE system allowed SLT at the school to attach custom flags (or tags) to students that would show up next to their names. This was immensely useful for teaching staff who needed to track whether students belong to certain groups and our schools are really missing this feature.

 Different schools could use these for lots of different purposes that aligned with their objectives, some examples:

 - School-wide focus students for assessment.

 - Students who are part of the school's mentoring programme.

 - Students being monitored for attendance.

 - Students with toilet passes.

 - Students with current safeguarding concerns.

 - Students with severe medical issues.

 - Students being celebrated for achievement (sports, house champions, etc.)

 These small icon flags would then appear next to students in lists, making it easy and quick to identify these students at a glance. For example, they would appear on registers, on the teaching group students list, and even optionally in reports. They would also appear at the top of a student's profile, similar to the it DFE indicators (PP, FSM, etc.) - Hovering over the flag would remind the user what that flag was with a small tooltip.

 Being able to identify students who have been flagged for various reasons by school leadership at a glance would save teachers a lot of time in learning and tracking who their students are, which is extremely useful for secondary schools where some teachers teach hundreds of students each week. These flags are consistent across the site and appear next to the student's name on registers, seating plans, class lists, profiles, reports, and even assessment entry. SLT are responsible for deciding what they want to track and put front-and-centre for their school staff.

 I feel like Arbor's existing *Custom Groups* feature is a really good base for this, especially with the ability to use rules for dynamic membership (e.g. attendance). Essentially, schools are _already_ able to flag their students in Arbor, but there's no nice quick and easy way to see whether students are members of those groups at a glance. All we would need is to be able to select an icon (from a small bank of pre-made icons or colours is fine) to represent that group, and choose whether we'd like this group to show up as a flag/tag to staff.

 We provided a small handful of icons in the old system, such as:

 [Flags of various colours]

 [Targets of various colours]

 [Tags of various colours]

 [Circled Letters: A, B, C, D]

 [Shapes / Square / Circle / Triangle / Star / etc.]

 [Icons for academic things: Trophy, Football, Calculator, Books, etc.]

 [Warning Symbol / Info Symbol]

 This covered a wide enough variety without needing to go to the hassle of making it possible for schools to upload their own icons or anything like that. Schools could pick an icon to attach to groups, along with a name (for the tooltip) and then put students in those groups to make the icons show up around the site. Simple but effective.

 I'd be happy to share a screenshot of this from our old system but it seems I cannot paste images into this text box!

comment: Currently, if a student is placed in a detention, this takes priority over other events. For example, if you then try to place a student who is already in a detention session of one form, Arbor will not allow you to put them into another one. It recognises that the student is unavailable and refuses to place them in the detention. However, it does not do the same with any other events. For example, we run a huge number of after-school intervention sessions that run at the same time as our detentions. Obviously, since the interventions are in place to help the students get to where they need to be academically, these sessions need to take priority over detentions. In an ideal world, I would create a regular repeating intervention session in the Interventions module, add the students we require, and then if a teacher assigns a detention to one of those students, it would recognise that they are in an intervention and that time and bump them on to the following day's detention instead. However, this does not happen and the students get added to the detention despite being unavailable. This has caused havoc with our detention attendance and chasing absentees. Our only solution has been to ignore the Interventions module altogether for anything that occurs after school, and instead create the intervention sessions as repeating detentions (you will see we have a huge number). In order to prevent students being added to a detention when they should be in intervention, this is the only method we can find. It means that I spend an entire working day every half term manually adding each of our students to the relevant intervention session for each week (rather than adding them in bulk for the whole term as I would in the Interventions module). We have a year 11 cohort of ~120 and on average, each student is in 3-4 interventions per week - each name has to be added manually for each session and it's the biggest headache in the world. While our level of interventions is extreme, I find it hard to believe that no other schools have similar sessions running concurrently with detentions and face the same dilemma of students being prioritised in a detention, rather than what is essentially a curriculum lesson.
journeys: Logging Behaviour and Rewards|Managing End-of-Day Dismissals and Clubs|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Logging Behaviour and Setting Detentions

comment:

comment:
Currently we are only able to add attendance notes to a lesson that is currently happening.

 We often have students who are present in school, but may not be in their lesson, for example, doing a speaking and listening exam for 15 minutes during a lesson.

 We found it helpful with our previous system to add a note in advance to the lesson the student would be missing so that the teacher was aware that they were still on site, but not in their lesson for a short period of time.

 As the student does not often miss the whole lesson, and is still present at school, using a planned absence is not necessarily the best action, it would be better if we could add an attendance note in advance of the event so that it is there on the day for the teacher to view. From an admin perspective, it is also easier to add in advance rather than when the lesson is happening as this is time consuming and could mean that we do not have time to add all the notes before the lesson is over.
journeys: Taking Period-by-Period Registers

The detention module in Arbor is great how it firstly links to the attendance on the day (pulling that through for each pupil) and then separating out the 'Absent - Not in school' and 'Absent - Failed to Attend', however, what would be a huge time saver for us is if the same drop down menu of upcoming detentions would be available in the Failed to attend list like it does in the not in school list. We would then be able to allocate a 'Failed to attend' behaviour and then select on the drop down the next appropriate upscaled detention.

comment:
Add a corrections setting to 'close' off or pause detentions/corrections so they do not continue to add to a specific session if it already appears to be getting full.

comment:
When attempting to put in a planned absence for a pupil, such as a medical appointment or part day absence etc, Arbor will not allow the planned absence to be added if there is already a "planned absence" set such as an Ad-hoc Intervention.

 It is very frustrating completing all the sections for the planned absence, clicking to save it, for it to be rejected and having to cancel it, go and find the planned absence already added for the date/time, deleting this and then going back to adding the planned absence that I needed to add.

 This wastes so much time and can be very frustrating, especially in the morning when Attendance is extremely busy.

 This also happens when added planned absences for part-time pupils over a date range. This is even more time consuming!

 There should be a Prompt box appear whenever a planned absence is being added that pops up when clicking save and giving the user an option to delete the original absence and over ride it.

comment:
Emergency Evacuation Registers need to print one form group to a page. They currently print over 1 to 2 pages and the second page does not have the name of the form group on.

 We are a relatively small secondary school with approx. 606 students on roll (24 form groups). During a fire drill several admin staff take a handful and registers over to the evacuation point to distribute. Because the registers print over 1-2 pages and the second page has no group information we are having to staple the sheets before distribution - in the event of a real fire this would not be possible. Also, if a second page was misplaced or unattached from the form group it belonged to, it could result in students not being accounted for. This poses a serious health and safety risk.

summary: Facility to allow copying of seating plans between classrooms
comment:
For some of the classes I teach, I use up to three different rooms, each with an identical seating layout. This leaves me in a quandary regarding seating plans.

 Eg. I teach 9B4 in 3 classrooms over the fortnight. This class most definitely requires a seating plan, and this is changed on say a monthly basis. Each classroom has exactly the same layout. At present, Arbor does not have a facility to copy/paste seating plans for this same class across each of the 3 classrooms. This means that if I change a seating plan for one classroom, I then have to manually change the other classrooms plans, which when a significant change has taken place, this task could take far far longer than needed, and opens up the chance of input error, In addition ,when a classroom change is required to suit the needs of the school, the seating plan for that room is wiped clean, leaving a blank template for a new classroom - therefore, please allow a facility whereby a class can be given a single seating plan anchored onto their dashboard despite the changing classrooms.
journeys: Managing Classroom Seating Plans|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Designing and Managing Seating Plans|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

Having spoken to the help desk, there is currently no way for me to print a weekly register for our preschool class where the sessions they are not due to attend are indicated in some way. comment:
Request:

 Is there a way that we run a report which gives the students individually even if they were logged together? So when I create a pivot table we don‚Äôt have separate lines for groups of students etc.

 Reply:

 As the report in the link below reports on Behaviour by Incident, all Students linked to the Incident will be included in the same line, and there isn‚Äôt a way to separate these in the report. A report with a Student focus will list the Students separately, but unfortunately there are very limited Behaviour fields that can be included in a Student report, and none of the columns you have used in your existing Behaviour Incident report are available.

 Arbor‚Äôs guidance points Schools towards the reporting options within the Behaviour area itself, although I appreciate that this might not give you all of the fields you require either. Further information on this can be found here: https://support.arbor-education.com/hc/en-us/articles/360007336658-Behaviour-Analysis-Reporting-on-incidents-detentions-exclusions-and-suspensions

 A school needs to be have to have group incidents but when reporting/ analysing these need to count against the individual student not separate lines for each group incident. It sways the data and doesn't give an accurate report of each individual student as some of their behaviour is on other lines. We want a report of every student and their incidents at a point in time.

comment:
Hi

 Your Bradford Factor calculation needs to be changed.

 Currently your Calculation:

 The score is calculated using the formula: Bradford Factor = (Number of Absences)¬≤ x Total Days Absent.

 It would be more useful:-

 the number of absence days squared multiplied by the number of incidences of absence.

 Named after Bradford University where it originated in the 1980s, the Bradford Factor is based on the theory that shorter, more frequent absences are more detrimental to an organization than longer, less frequent ones.

 This is also true in schools, frequent five odd days off are more detrimental than one week period of absence.

 Identifying the odd days off is the purpose of the Bradford factor so the underlying causes can be addressed.

 Please can you change your calculation.

summary: Trip Registers to be counted as statutory attendance
comment:
Good morning,

Arbor states school trips does not count towards statutory attendance and does not affect lesson registers. It relies on teachers doing a manual, remote entry or adding a planned absence. This is a safeguarding issue.

For safeguarding and accurate recording of attendance Trips should be statutory attendance as they are during the school day and we have a duty of care for the students. There is already the facility to create a trip register and to take the register so we just need the marks to pull through, akin to Intervention registers. This would mean it creates a separate register, as it does for C2 and B registers. V is a statutory mark.
Alternatively, if you decide they are not statutory, add a toggle to the trip register to carry them over if required and this would then be just one click for us to carry the marks across.

Not pulling the mark through creates double/triple work and opens it up to error and safeguarding concerns.

Would you please see if this can be rectified?

Many thanks
journeys: Accessing Vital Pupil Context|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Accessing Vital Pupil Context and SEND Passports

summary: Use of reasonable  force and other  restrictive  interventions in  schools Guidance for schools in England February 2025
comment:
DFE released Statutory document for Use of reasonable force and other
restrictive interventions in schools Guidance for schools in England
February 2025. This needs to be in place for September please read link below. I need this to feature on Arbor to enable staff to log and report.

I need Arbor to have on their logging and reporting system for Physical Interventions the following minimum:

Schools should record the following details as a minimum:

• names of pupil and staff directly involved
• any relevant needs or circumstances of the pupil, including whether the pupil
involved has an identified special educational need or disability and their SEN
status code
• time, date, location and approximate length of time the intervention was used
• brief account of the incident, including what led up to the incident, identified or
potential triggers if known, any preventative or de-escalation strategies used, what
type of reasonable force was applied, the degree of force, and details of physical
injuries sustained, if applicable
• brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance
• any post-incident support, such as details of any medical treatment for injuries or other adverse impacts

Schools may also wish to record additional details to support their evaluation of incidents to identify best practices and areas for improvement. For example, the pupil’s and/or witnesses’ accounts of what happened, when and how parents were notified, and what
follow-up has taken place.

A report of the incident made to parents should include the following details as a minimum:
• time, date, location and approximate length of time the force was used
• brief account of what type of reasonable force was applied, and the degree of force
• details of any physical injuries sustained, if applicable
• brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

Thank you.
Ryan Storey
Isebrook School
Assistant Headteacher
01536 500030

https://consult.education.gov.uk/behaviour-unit/revised-use-of-reasonable-force-guidance/supporting_documents/Use%20of%20reasonable%20force%20and%20other%20restrictive%20interventions%20guidance.pdf

comment:

We run a lot of trips, some of which last over multiple days. There is not yet the ability to take a register for each day of a multi-day trip, which is a shame! For example, we have set a trip up on Arbor for a trip to The Watersports Academy, where the students are visiting every day this week. summary: Dynamic Timetable Cycle Management for Secondary School
comment:
1. Multi-Year Group Support
Description: The system should allow the creation of separate timetable cycles for each key stage or year group. This will ensure that KS3 (Years 7-9), KS4 (Years 10-11), and KS5 (Years 12-13) can all have different schedules, taking into account their unique subject offerings and requirements.

Key Features:
Year Group-Specific Cycles: Each year group can have a customized cycle, with its own timetable structure, subject offerings, lesson durations, and break times.

2. Flexible Timetable Cycles (Weekly, Bi-Weekly)
Description: A dynamic timetable allows flexibility in how lessons are scheduled across different weeks or terms. For example, a school might want different cycles for each key stage to ensure students are exposed to a variety of subjects at different times of day.

Key Features:
Cycle Scheduling: The ability to define weekly, bi-weekly schedules for different year groups, and to change cycles based on school needs (e.g., for exam periods or extracurricular activities).

3. Breaks and Lunch Customization
Description: Breaks and lunch times might differ for different year groups to avoid overcrowding in common areas. The system should be able to customize break and lunch periods based on the year group.

Key Features:
Customizable Break/Lunch Times: Allow different year groups to have different break and lunch periods, ensuring efficient use of school facilities and reducing overcrowding.

Having different time slots for different KS giving the error in printable timetable. Student is getting confuse with their timetable when it is printed. They are not able to see properly. If you need any help with implementation I am happy to share it.
journeys: Taking Period-by-Period Registers

comment:
At present Arbor detentions only work in a horizontal fashion:

James gets a detention period 1 on Monday morning - this automatically sets the detention for Monday after school
James then gets another detention on Monday period 2 - he gets another detention set for Tuesday
James gets a further detention for being disruptive during period 3 - this gives him another detention on Wednesday

It would be really helpful if it could also work in a vertical hierarchy, therefore stacking the detentions.

Monday period 1 - 30 mins detention
Monday period 2 - 30 mins detention
Monday period 3 - 30 mins detention

This would give James a 90 minute detention on Monday after school.

comment:
This is a issue that I have encountered that is an error with Arbor and needs correcting.
I've discovered a problem with absences reported via the parent portal and AI parent messages for pupils who are on work experience (23rd  June - 4th July).
I created a planned absence for YR10 for these 2 weeks and coded "W".
Over the past 2 weeks, parents have notified us of their child's absence.  This has been fine when I've received the information via an email to attendance, as I've just coded the pupil absence, with the corresponding code, for the day via "Bulk Edit Marks Standard".

However, when I've accepted absences via the parent portal and AI parent messages, the absence has NOT translated to the pupil on their attendance and the code has stayed as "W".
I only discovered this yesterday, when I noticed that a child who had been off sick for the week and the absence accepted daily via the Arbor parent messaging , was still coded "W", when I'd accepted the absence every day and changed the code to "I".  I went through a few pupils who I knew had been off for multiple days and had to change their coding as they were the same, still coded "W".  There are lots more that will need doing unfortunately.

Even though, I've changed the code to either "I", "M", "O" etc and it's been accepted, it's not actually worked for any pupil. Not only have the codes not changed, but the message from the parent, is also lost as that hasn't copied across either.

What is frustrating is that Arbor did not alert me to the fact that it wouldn't accept the absence at the time (like it does every time you try and put in a planned absence but the child already has a random ad-hoc intervention!).

Nothing was flagged, Arbor accepted the absence this way and now I have to go back through all the messages by searching for them and changing them again.

To say this is frustrating is an understatement as well as doubling workload. Not impressed.
journeys: Parent and guardian messages and notes|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

summary: Recording Physical intervention - URGENT Update required
comment:
From September 2025 it will become necessary for all schools to record the details of a physical intervention. While Arbor currently has a method to do this it does not cover all the areas that will now be required to be record most significantly the amount of time a pupil was in hold for.  This particularly necessary for SEN schools.
If this is not updated in Arbor then the current recording process becomes void.
A snippet follows from the Use of reasonable force and other restrictive interventions in schools - Guidance for schools in England - February 2025
Schools should record the following details as a minimum:
• names of pupil and staff directly involved
• any relevant needs or circumstances of the pupil, including whether the pupil involved has an identified special educational need or disability and their SEN status code
• time, date, location and approximate length of time the intervention was used
• brief account of the incident, including what led up to the incident, identified or
potential triggers if known, any preventative or de-escalation strategies used, what
type of reasonable force was applied, the degree of force, and details of physical
injuries sustained, if applicable
• brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance
• any post-incident support, such as details of any medical treatment for injuries or
other adverse impacts

I would like a method to bulk create or clone programme modules with classes & lessons to speed up two tasks.

The first is to create a single programme, and then to bulk create modules inside that programme that have the same repeating classes & lessons but could then have different students.  The names of the modules could start as 1, 2, 3 etc and be changed manually and the rooms and staff could be added through Timetable slots.  This would be used when an entire year group drops out of normal lessons for a day into reg groups for enrichment modules, or on the last day of the year when we need a one off extra tutor register etc.

The second is to create a programme and bulk create modules inside that programme that have repeating classes for every period of the school calendar, e.g. Amon1, Amon2 etc, that students could be added to when they are not following some part of their normal calendar..  This would be used for students that drop a subject to attend a nurture room or fixed study period. role: Data and Systems Manager, Senior Leader
comment:
From our recent parents survey, a significant number of concerns have been raised in regards to the Arbor Parent App and its functionality. We used to have Edulink (with SIMS) before moving to Arbor and introducing the Arbor Parent App. The Parent App has come out as one of two main areas of concern and frustration of parents. Please see below the feedback - your assistance in improving this would be much appreciated!!!

- in-app message emails sent to parents without the app installed are poor. Should be replaced by email with actual content, or SMS
- no Google Classroom homework integration (Edulink provides this)
- absence reporting not as good
- parents evening - Edulink provides this as virtual parents evenings free of charge, not possible in Arbor so we have had to purchase School Cloud
- catering information poor (integration with ParentPay not great)
- lay-out of pages not easy, having to click into multiple sections to find info about child

We are at a stage now where we are likely to withdraw from the Parent App and return to Edulink. I really don't want to do this as I want to keep everything in one place but the amount of very valid opinions on this is too large to ignore.

summary: Beng able to add multiple house points to groups of students
comment: We have a robust system for house point awards in our school and students really value the rewards.  However we are all finding since migrating to Arbor that we're putting fewer on, so fewer children are  earning the rewards and this is having the effect of undermining our reward programme.  Putting multiple house points on for multiple students is quite a laborious process.  I think there is an easy fix though.  We can put a house point or behaviour category on for multiple students but we can't put multiple behaviour categories in.  The drop down box has the appearance as if this were possible, but if you try to add a second behaviour category, the first one disappears and you have to put all the students in again to do a second entry.  So for example if I have 10 students in a class and at the end of the lesson I want to award 3 different categories of house point (e.g  a positive, respectful and engaged point) I have to select all the students 3 times.  If I could select the students once and then add all three house point categories in the behaviour box, this would save lots of time and I think would incentivise staff to put more house points on for students.   I hope you can help us with this.
journeys: Logging Behaviour and Rewards|Logging Behaviour and Setting Detentions|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

summary: Behaviour set up and work flow
comment:
In order to create a workflow to trigger an alert for certificates etc - positive behaviours current need to be within 'Incidents' - we are not comfortable with positive points being in this area due to the negative connotations of the word 'incident' - when we tried to use this function both students and parents fedback to say this was inapppropriate and we ended up going back to classcharts. We have tried again to swithc our behaviour to Arbor now that students and parents have visibility and can see points awarded, however there are still too many barriers for it to work for us.

We have added positive point categories to this area - which is great as they all show on the 'my classroom view' to enable staff to award them quickly in the lesson. Hwever on the 'incident types' you can only have 6 shoing at any time and have to search for any others - this relies on staff remmebering exactly what they are called and takes extra time.

So an improvement we would like to see asap if possible is:
- unlimited categories of 'Types' of incident to show through on the 'my classroom' seating plan view
- For the positive points are to work in the same way as the 'Types' do enabling a workflow to be created to trigger a milestone alert.
- If the 'types' doesn't allow a workflow behind the scenes either (we currently do this in classcharts) we need it to also have a workflow, which can lead to it triggering a detention at the next possible event - addin it to the student's calendar.

Until these changes are made - we are stuck using 2 systems and having our behviour system in one system (classcharts) and everythig else in Arbor which is creating additional workload for staff and annoyance for parents having to check in 2 different places. Having bheviour in Arbor would also encourage students to log in and use it more as currenlty they rely on their outlook calendar for lessons and then look at behviour on classcharts - navigating around the need to log into Arbor other than for homework. We are an iPad school, so it is frustrating they are not using the Arbor student portal.

comment:

As an example - if I have whole year group immunisations for Y8's, I need to know where each student is for each period across the school day.

This information is needed in excel format as I can create conditional formatting for consent/no consent, ensuring who has been collected, who is still needing to be collected etc.  At the moment, I have to download each register individually for each class across each period as there is no option to bulk download into excel, only into PDF format.

comment: When adding planned absences there is no option to override a clash with an existing planned absence.  Instead a pop up message states that the absence cannot be logged because a planned absence already exists.  This is particularly frustrating as we have to then find the original planned absence to override it manually.  It would be more useful and time efficient if the pop up gave the option to review and override the clash.  For example someone may be marked out as code V on one day for an upcoming trip, they then might be taken ill, have surgery etc, and need to be marked out for the entire week, clearly they will no longer be going on the trip so we need to override the V to an I.  This is doable only by finding the original planned absence, which in a school of over 800 students, we might not know to look for until we have tried to log the illness and get the error pop up. summary: Statutory attendance widget should not include Year 11 for second half of Summer Attendance
role: Head of Data, Insight and Analysis
comment:
Afternoon!

Summer Half Term Year 11 data shouldn't be reported in the widget for statutory attendance on the main school dashboard. This guidance can be found in the DfE methodology here: https://explore-education-statistics.service.gov.uk/methodology/pupil-absence-statistics-methodology (Data Collection section, under section 4.4.1 > Summer half term) - which explains that Year 11 data is collected but not reported.

Seeing the numbers in Arbor puts a perverse incentive on our staff to fiddle the figures to make them better, e.g. off-rolling pupils early, or recording them as B or # when they should really be S.

It would be amazing if you could align the Statutory widget with DfE methodology for Year 11. Thank you!

comment:
Hi,

I hope you're well.

Would it be possible to implement a register cut-off time for staff? Our registration period runs from 8:40 a.m. to 9:00 a.m., and we would like to restrict staff access to amend register marks after 9:00 a.m. This would ensure that any late arrivals are managed solely by the office team.

Currently, the register remains open to staff from 8:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. for the morning session and 12:30 p.m. to 3:00 p.m. for the afternoon session. We believe reintroducing a similar cut-off mechanism would help maintain accuracy and consistency in our attendance records.

Kind regards,
Ian

comment:
Suggested Improvement: Automatic Sanctions in Behaviour Workflows

It would be extremely helpful if, as part of the behaviour workflow setup, schools could automatically schedule a sanction (e.g. detention) when a student reaches a defined number of incidents for a particular behaviour type.

Many schools issue detentions for repeated minor behaviours (such as incorrect uniform or missed homework). Currently, this can only be managed by creating a separate "detention" workflow that other workflows escalate to after a certain number of incidents. However, this approach means that each type of detention (e.g. lunchtime, after-school, subject-specific) requires its own dedicated workflow.

Since Arbor currently allows a maximum of five behaviour workflows, using several for detention allocation severely limits a school’s ability to create other useful workflows for different behaviour types.

A more flexible system that allows automatic sanction scheduling within a single workflow – triggered by behaviour frequency thresholds – would make this much more efficient and allow schools to manage behaviour escalation without compromising workflow capacity.

comment:
Overview:
Introduce a dedicated Accident Reporting feature within the parent app that allows staff to quickly log incidents (e.g., trips, falls, cuts), record any first aid applied, and automatically notify parents/guardians in real time.

Why This Feature?
Critical for SEN Schools: These settings require rigorous, transparent, and timely communication around student well-being. A centralised digital record is invaluable.

Saves Time: Eliminates the need for paper forms or multiple communication steps.

Increased Parent Trust: Parents stay informed immediately and can review full details at their convenience.

Audit Trail: Provides a searchable history of incidents, including timestamps and staff involved—useful for both internal review and external reporting.

Key Functionalities:
Quick Logging Interface for Staff

Type of incident (trip, fall, bump, cut, etc.)

Time & location

Action taken (e.g., first aid, ice pack, monitored)

Optional notes or photo upload (e.g., minor scrape, playground location)

Auto-Notification to Parents

Instant in-app push notification

Summary view + full report available in the app

Parent View

Timeline of incident

What happened, what action was taken, by whom

Acknowledgement button (optional)

Admin Dashboard

Filter by date, student, incident type

Export reports (e.g., for school health & safety logs)

Benefits:
Enhances child safety protocols

Reduces admin burden

Builds trust with parents

Supports legal and safeguarding compliance

comment:
Hi,

We really need their to be an opportunity to add different coloured flags against students. Currently we can only add red.

When search the internet it suggested the below. Can this be done please?

Step 1: Create a User Defined Field (UDF)
Navigate to:
System > User Defined Fields

Select Add User Defined Field.

Complete the configuration:

Related to: Select Students

Field Type: Select Drop-down

Field Name/Identifier: Enter Focus Five

Options:

Yes

(Optional: Add No or leave blank as default)

Under Permissions, ensure that appropriate staff (e.g. designated SLT or Data Team) can access and edit the UDF.

Click Save to finalise the field.

This step establishes the backend field required to drive the visual flag.

Step 2: Create a Linked Visual Flag
Still in the System area, locate either:

School Settings > Student Flags

or System > Configuration > Flags (depending on your Arbor version)

Click New Flag.

Complete the flag setup:

Name: Focus Five Flag

Description: e.g. Used to identify students in termly Focus Five cohorts

Type: Select User Defined Field

Linked UDF Field: Choose the Focus Five drop-down created in Step 1

Display Icon/Colour: Choose a distinct colour and icon that does not conflict with existing flags (e.g. green circle)

Tooltip (optional): Focus Five student – termly support initiative

Active: Ensure this option is ticked

Click Create to finalise the visual flag.

This step ensures the system will display a coloured flag automatically when a student’s UDF is set to “Yes.”

Step 3: Apply the Flag to Students
Option A: Individual Student Assignment
Navigate to the relevant student profile.

Scroll to the Custom Fields or User Defined Fields section.

Set the Focus Five field to Yes.

Save the change.

Confirm that the Focus Five flag now appears in the student profile header.

Option B: Bulk Assignment
Navigate to:
System > User Defined Fields

Select the Focus Five field from the list.

Use the Bulk Actions > Edit Values tool.

Select the students to be included in the Focus Five cohort.

Set their field value to Yes and apply changes.

This method is recommended for termly updates.

Step 4: Confirm Flag Visibility
To verify the flag is functioning correctly:

Open a register for a class containing a Focus Five student. The coloured flag should appear next to their name.

In My Classroom, open a seating plan. The flag should appear in the student badge area.

Within a student profile, the flag will be visible in the header section once the UDF is set to “Yes.”

If flags are not immediately visible, a system refresh or overnight sync may be required.

We do no have this option on Arbor currently.

summary: Trip Registers to be counted as statutory attendance
comment:
Good morning,

 Arbor states school trips does not count towards statutory attendance and does not affect lesson registers. It relies on teachers doing a manual, remote entry or adding a planned absence. This is a safeguarding issue.

 For safeguarding and accurate recording of attendance Trips should be statutory attendance as they are during the school day and we have a duty of care for the students. There is already the facility to create a trip register and to take the register so we just need the marks to pull through, akin to Intervention registers. This would mean it creates a separate register, as it does for C2 and B registers. V is a statutory mark.

 Alternatively, if you decide they are not statutory, add a toggle to the trip register to carry them over if required and this would then be just one click for us to carry the marks across.

 Not pulling the mark through creates double/triple work and opens it up to error and safeguarding concerns.

 Would you please see if this can be rectified?

 Many thanks

summary: Use of reasonable force and other restrictive interventions in schools Guidance for schools in England February 2025
comment:
DFE released Statutory document for Use of reasonable force and other

 restrictive interventions in schools Guidance for schools in England

 February 2025. This needs to be in place for September please read link below. I need this to feature on Arbor to enable staff to log and report.

 I need Arbor to have on their logging and reporting system for Physical Interventions the following minimum:

 Schools should record the following details as a minimum:

 ‚Ä¢ names of pupil and staff directly involved

 ‚Ä¢ any relevant needs or circumstances of the pupil, including whether the pupil

 involved has an identified special educational need or disability and their SEN

 status code

 ‚Ä¢ time, date, location and approximate length of time the intervention was used

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of the incident, including what led up to the incident, identified or

 potential triggers if known, any preventative or de-escalation strategies used, what

 type of reasonable force was applied, the degree of force, and details of physical

 injuries sustained, if applicable

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

 ‚Ä¢ any post-incident support, such as details of any medical treatment for injuries or other adverse impacts

 Schools may also wish to record additional details to support their evaluation of incidents to identify best practices and areas for improvement. For example, the pupil‚Äôs and/or witnesses‚Äô accounts of what happened, when and how parents were notified, and what

 follow-up has taken place.

 A report of the incident made to parents should include the following details as a minimum:

 ‚Ä¢ time, date, location and approximate length of time the force was used

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of what type of reasonable force was applied, and the degree of force

 ‚Ä¢ details of any physical injuries sustained, if applicable

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

 Thank you.

 Ryan Storey

 Isebrook School

 Assistant Headteacher

 01536 500030

 https://consult.education.gov.uk/behaviour-unit/revised-use-of-reasonable-force-guidance/supporting_documents/Use%20of%20reasonable%20force%20and%20other%20restrictive%20interventions%20guidance.pdf

Exam Access Arrangements;

 However the descriptions for Computer reader / reader and Read aloud / examination reading pen often get confused for two reasons: The descriptions and the shortening of them.

 Firstly I think it should be Reader/ computer reader and Examination pen/Read aloud as I think this may reflect the more frequently used Access Arrangement. or Human reader/ Computer reader

summary: Dynamic Timetable Cycle Management for Secondary School
comment:
1. Multi-Year Group Support

 Description: The system should allow the creation of separate timetable cycles for each key stage or year group. This will ensure that KS3 (Years 7-9), KS4 (Years 10-11), and KS5 (Years 12-13) can all have different schedules, taking into account their unique subject offerings and requirements.

 Key Features:

 Year Group-Specific Cycles: Each year group can have a customized cycle, with its own timetable structure, subject offerings, lesson durations, and break times.

 2. Flexible Timetable Cycles (Weekly, Bi-Weekly)

 Description: A dynamic timetable allows flexibility in how lessons are scheduled across different weeks or terms. For example, a school might want different cycles for each key stage to ensure students are exposed to a variety of subjects at different times of day.

 Key Features:

 Cycle Scheduling: The ability to define weekly, bi-weekly schedules for different year groups, and to change cycles based on school needs (e.g., for exam periods or extracurricular activities).

 3. Breaks and Lunch Customization

 Description: Breaks and lunch times might differ for different year groups to avoid overcrowding in common areas. The system should be able to customize break and lunch periods based on the year group.

 Key Features:

 Customizable Break/Lunch Times: Allow different year groups to have different break and lunch periods, ensuring efficient use of school facilities and reducing overcrowding.

comment:
This is a issue that I have encountered that is an error with Arbor and needs correcting.

 I've discovered a problem with absences reported via the parent portal and AI parent messages for pupils who are on work experience (23rd June - 4th July).

 I created a planned absence for YR10 for these 2 weeks and coded "W".

 Over the past 2 weeks, parents have notified us of their child's absence. This has been fine when I've received the information via an email to attendance, as I've just coded the pupil absence, with the corresponding code, for the day via "Bulk Edit Marks Standard".

 However, when I've accepted absences via the parent portal and AI parent messages, the absence has NOT translated to the pupil on their attendance and the code has stayed as "W".

 I only discovered this yesterday, when I noticed that a child who had been off sick for the week and the absence accepted daily via the Arbor parent messaging , was still coded "W", when I'd accepted the absence every day and changed the code to "I". I went through a few pupils who I knew had been off for multiple days and had to change their coding as they were the same, still coded "W". There are lots more that will need doing unfortunately.

 Even though, I've changed the code to either "I", "M", "O" etc and it's been accepted, it's not actually worked for any pupil. Not only have the codes not changed, but the message from the parent, is also lost as that hasn't copied across either.

 What is frustrating is that Arbor did not alert me to the fact that it wouldn't accept the absence at the time (like it does every time you try and put in a planned absence but the child already has a random ad-hoc intervention!).

 Nothing was flagged, Arbor accepted the absence this way and now I have to go back through all the messages by searching for them and changing them again.

 To say this is frustrating is an understatement as well as doubling workload. Not impressed.

summary: Recording Physical intervention - URGENT Update required
comment:
From September 2025 it will become necessary for all schools to record the details of a physical intervention. While Arbor currently has a method to do this it does not cover all the areas that will now be required to be record most significantly the amount of time a pupil was in hold for. This particularly necessary for SEN schools.

 If this is not updated in Arbor then the current recording process becomes void.

 A snippet follows from the Use of reasonable force and other restrictive interventions in schools - Guidance for schools in England - February 2025

 Schools should record the following details as a minimum:

 ‚Ä¢ names of pupil and staff directly involved

 ‚Ä¢ any relevant needs or circumstances of the pupil, including whether the pupil involved has an identified special educational need or disability and their SEN status code

 ‚Ä¢ time, date, location and approximate length of time the intervention was used

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of the incident, including what led up to the incident, identified or

 potential triggers if known, any preventative or de-escalation strategies used, what

 type of reasonable force was applied, the degree of force, and details of physical

 injuries sustained, if applicable

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

 ‚Ä¢ any post-incident support, such as details of any medical treatment for injuries or

 other adverse impacts

summary: statutory guidance from DFE - Physical Intervention Report and Recording
comment:
https://consult.education.gov.uk/behaviour-unit/revised-use-of-reasonable-force-guidance/supporting_documents/Use%20of%20reasonable%20force%20and%20other%20restrictive%20interventions%20guidance.pdf

 Arbor needs to have this correct for September.

 Recording and reporting the use of force by members

 of staff

 Recording the use of force (statutory guidance)

 Governing bodies of maintained schools and the proprietors of other schools must

 ensure that a procedure is in place for recording each significant incident in which a

 member of staff uses force on a pupil, as part of the school‚Äôs duty under section 93A of

 the Education and Inspections Act 2006.

 Incidents must be recorded by the staff member(s) involved as soon as practicable after

 the event.

 The procedure must require that a record of any such incident is made in writing as soon

 as practicable after the incident. The requirement to record applies even if the use of

 reasonable force and other restrictive interventions in certain circumstances is agreed

 with parents as part of a pupil‚Äôs behaviour support plan.

 In discharging their duty to have a procedure in place, the governing body or proprietor

 must have regard to this guidance and any other relevant guidance issued by the

 Secretary of State for these purposes.

 Schools should record the following details as a minimum:

 ‚Ä¢ names of pupil and staff directly involved

 ‚Ä¢ any relevant needs or circumstances of the pupil, including whether the pupil

 involved has an identified special educational need or disability and their SEN

 status code

 ‚Ä¢ time, date, location and approximate length of time the intervention was used

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of the incident, including what led up to the incident, identified or

 potential triggers if known, any preventative or de-escalation strategies used, what

 type of reasonable force was applied, the degree of force, and details of physical

 injuries sustained, if applicable

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

 ‚Ä¢ any post-incident support, such as details of any medical treatment for injuries or

 other adverse impacts

 Schools may also wish to record additional details to support their evaluation of incidents

 to identify best practices and areas for improvement. For example, the pupil‚Äôs and/or

 witnesses‚Äô accounts of what happened, when and how parents were notified, and what

 follow-up has taken place.

 15

 Reporting the use of force (statutory guidance)

 Governing bodies of maintained schools and the proprietors of other schools must

 ensure that a procedure is in place for reporting each significant incident in which a

 member of staff uses force on a pupil to each parent of the pupil as soon as practicable

 after the incident, except where:

 ‚Ä¢ the pupil is aged 20 or over; or

 ‚Ä¢ it appears to the staff member that doing so would be likely to result in significant

 harm to the pupil. This includes all forms of abuse and neglect. In this instance,

 the staff member must report the incident to the local authority within whose area

 the pupil is ordinarily resident.

 A report of the incident made to parents should include the following details as a

 minimum:

 ‚Ä¢ time, date, location and approximate length of time the force was used

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of what type of reasonable force was applied, and the degree of

 force

 ‚Ä¢ details of any physical injuries sustained, if applicable

 ‚Ä¢ brief account of why the use of force was assessed as necessary in that instance

 The requirement to report applies even if the use of reasonable force in certain

 circumstances is agreed with parents as part of a pupil‚Äôs behaviour support plan.

 Schools may choose how best to communicate this information to parents. For example,

 via phone call, email or online messaging system. Best practice would include inviting

 parents to have a follow-up discussion about the incident where appropriate. This could

 involve a discussion about any behavioural triggers, any warning signs of an impending

 incident, whether any agreed behaviour support plans were followed, what de-escalation

 strategies were used and how effective they were, and what might be done differently in

 the future. The school may use this information to amend any existing behaviour support

 plans, as needed.

summary: Improvement to parents booking after school activity clubs
comment:
We currently send letters out to parents promoting after school clubs each half term and as I'm sure you can imagine this is time consuming and therefore we would like to use Arbor and encourage parents to book clubs on behalf of their child/ren. We have two members of staff that have children at our school and therefore we have used them as guinea pigs to determine how the club works from a parent's point of view and to establish what happens if the club is full.

 We created a test club and added our colleagues' two children and set the maximum participants to '1'. One colleague booked their child onto the club successfully, however, when the second colleague tried to book on her phone, she could get so far on the app until 'Select Membership Periods' at which point the screen went blank with a green button at the bottom and she couldn't go any further. I logged onto this colleague's account on a desk top computer to see if it is different and again, it allows me to register her child up until 'Select Membership Periods' - at this point there are no details to select in the drop down menu. If I click on the 'Confirm Registration' button, a box appears asking for the parent to consent and if I select 'Yes' the 'Select membership Periods' is highlighted in red but this is blank as mentioned above - so parents cannot get any further.

 I contacted Arbor support and was told the issues we are experiencing are because the club is at capacity.

 Unfortunately, due to this we are unable to use the clubs function for parents to book directly as it will cause more problems and is frustrating, it also looks like a technical glitch. A better solution would be for parents to see a message stating that the club is full and they have been added to a waiting list or to register their child on a separate waiting list (created in a similar manner as the main clubs).

 What next?

 Jade Taylor
