summary: Colours in Behaviour charts are fixed and therefore unhelpful - Negative points are showing as green, positive as red.
comment:
Despite directionality being set up correctly within Point Awards, the colours that each point appears in on behaviour graphs is fixed. This is causing a lot of confusion within our staff body as, visually, it looks like students in this school have more negative than positive as the natural association with these colours are green=good, red/orange=bad.

 PLEASE allow users to choose the colour for the 5 categories that are shown in each Reg Form's behaviour graph: Positive Incidents, Negative Incidents, Negative Points, Positive Points, Detentions.

 Even if you have to keep the default Arbor colour scheme, please can we choose which of these colours we assign to which category. Both shades of green work for positive points and incidents, and I could cope with yellow and orange for negative points and incidents. Red for detentions is fine.

 But at the moment, our 'negative points' is currently green. I spoke to Arbor support about this and they said it will likely be related to the order the points were set up. In one of our other schools (I work for the MAT), Positive points is green so this makes for a graph that makes more visual sense. There is no indicator when setting up point awards that creating one before the other would impact these colours; I thought this would be tied to 'directionality' which makes a bit more sense, but it isn't! It's related to a fixed order which is quite arbitrary as it's likely a 50/50 split between people who put create negative first and people who create positive first - this is random and should not have an impact on anything fixed that they can't change, especially when choosing a 'directionality' is an option. I'd even accept directionality choice dictating the colours.

 This difference in colours across our schools also makes comparison virtually impossible as the graph colours look different across each school. Some have lots of green, some have no green whatsoever, some have mostly orange - no consistent key can be applied across schools and visually it cannot be interpreted for multiple schools at the same time when they appear so differently. And we don't just want to delete the existing point awards to create them in a different order as this will remove historic points, and we have reports etc tied to these points so this isn't an option for us.

 Thank you for reading my concerns; I really hope this is actioned as it's causing a lot of confusion amongst staff and stifling our behaviour comparison between our schools.

*+Current problem+*

 I am trying to set up a detention system and I have run into a problem with the way that Arbor handles detention double booking to next available session. The detention system works like a calendar where you cannot double book students. Anyone who gets multiple detentions for the same time will automatically be sent to the next available session of that type.

 E.g. Student X gets a 30 minute detention in one lesson, an hour in another and misses their homework earning them another 30 minutes. Because they are all for the same time, they sit the 30 minute behaviour one day, the hour the next day and the homework detention after that.

 Idea:

summary: Interventions - Not timetabled & Detentions Delay timings
comment:
Good afternoon,

 I would appreciate it that serious consideration is given to these two ideas.

 1. Recording interventions which are not timetable based. I would like to see a set up similar that of behaviour incident recording which allows for adhoc addition of interventions and support strategies which are not timetabled activities. For example, I would like to be able to customise a series of interventions which are assigned a tiered level.

 Examples - Tutor behaviour report started, Tutor behaviour report ended, Tutor behaviour report escaleted to Year Leader Report etc, EHCP Review meeting, Behaviour Meeting parent, IBP Commenced, IBP Review. IBP closed. IBP Escalated to PSP.

 These would be inputted to a similar data entry system as the behaviour logging.

 Then a timeline could be created of support provided to each pupil which can be combined with the timetabled interventions in the current system.

 2. I have requested this previously but has not been actioned. The 24hour delay on detentions does not work adequately for schools that set lunchtime detentions which are set in the afternoon. For example. If I set a pupil a lunch detention at 9am in the morning with a 24hr delay that lunch detention will be set for 12.00pm the following day. If I set the child a detention at 1.00pm on a Monday, the 24 hour delay will mean that detention will not be sat until Wednesday.

 The simple solution is to set a 12hr delay or a next day delay. At the moment I have to fudge the timings to accommodate this issue by telling the system the lunch detention is at 5pm and the afterschool detention is at 6.00pm.

 Would really appreciate some consideration of these suggestions and happy to discuss and explain in further detail.

comment:
Previously we had SIMS and we were able to take an emergency fire drill which was a register that could be taken in the event of a fire but had no impact on the actual registers.

 Staff would click on their class and select present, missing or absent. Once accounted for only the missing children would remain.

 The headteacher could look at the whole school and see which children were missing.

 If during a lockdown a child was in a different classroom the teacher could go to the other classes register and select that the child was present.

 This register can then be shared with the relevant emergency response team. If you click on the missing child it had their photo and also an emergency contact number.

 At the end of the drill you can just reset the register.

 It is hard to explain but I do have a process document I can share should you need the actual process that was involved.

 Currently with Arbor we are having to revert to a less efficient method which is not ideal.

summary: Service Child is NOT the same at Pupil Premium
comment:
Hi, these are 2 separate funds that we cannot use in the same way. It really complicates things when staff cannot rely on the PPG data for analysis or to know who is entitled to trip subsidies, etc. Please change this so Service Children are not automatically counted as PPG!! Here is the link to the guidance and here is the extract

 https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/the-service-pupil-premium/service-pupil-premium-what-you-need-to-know#:~:text=%C2%A9%20Crown%20copyright%202025,%C2%A3350%20per%20service%20child.

 **How SPP differs from the Pupil Premium (PP)**

 SPP helps schools provide mainly pastoral support for service children. The Pupil Premium (PP) was introduced to raise attainment and accelerate progress within disadvantaged groups.

 Schools should not combine SPP with PP funding: the spending of each premium should be accounted for separately, but schools can be allocated both SPP and PP for the same child if they are eligible for both.

 Thanks

 Natalie

comment:

 "Whilst that data has pulled through, I can confirm that the reason that the Planned Absence doesn't show up in the register is because of how it was created (for both students)

 This planned absence is classed as a narrative (note) associated with a planned absence and these two form of notes don't appear the same on registers.

 This means that planned absence notes won't show the same as lesson attendance notes but the best way to see how both of these display (and which is which) is on the Bulk Edit Marks Advanced page.

 You can also add notes from this page so the note will appear in Students > Attendance > Registers > Daily Attendance as you expected. "

comment:
Using a bit of react / javascript your team could change the seating plan builder to work in a similar way to class charts.

 As an end user, I have spent hours arranging seating plans in a manner that would have taken much less time with Class charts.

 1) Classcharts allows you to click on students on the seating plan and quickly view their SEN information (via the seating plan). This then allows the teacher to make quick decisions on moving students within the same view. Currently the Arbor App requires accessing student information via a separate window.

 2) Classcharts allows the teacher to drag students and move them to another position in the classroom. In Arbor I have to click where I want a student to go select them, then go to the position I have moved them from and select the student who has been displaced. This is far less agile than Classcharts.

 3) Classcharts allows teachers in the MyClassroom view to click on a student to allocate a house point (as does Arbor), however Arbor only lets you allocate multiple by going to a different view.

 Essentially you need to make the MyClassroom view work in the same manner as Classcharts. The majority of classroom teachers in the UK are expected to have quick access to a seating plan and be able to pull up SEN information about students due to quality assurance requirements from their management. Also, teachers will want to make quick adjustments to seating whilst teaching a whole class, being able to simply drag-and-drop as Classcharts does (and view specific SEN information whilst doing this) is better for the end use than the current setup Arbor features.

comment:
Hello,

 Currently in Arbor detentions are unable to run at the same time with an escalation policy. This means that, for example, we have two detentions that run every single day, and our escalation system does not allow them to be escalated from one detention to the next. If a student in our school receives a Level 3 negative, they are assigned an academy detention. However, if they are assigned another level 3 negative in the same day they should be escalated to a leadership detention. They way we have been able t do this is to mark detentions run at different times, but this is not a true representation of the times of the detention and causes further admin work in terms of communications to parents and staff. My idea is a way where arbor could implement a system where students are able to be removed automatically from one detention and added to the next if the escalation process allows this. This follows on from another idea I have presented regarding detention escalation. I think it would be highly beneficial if Arbor would allow detentions to run at the same times and overlap but students still be added to the correct detention and removed from the other one to make a smooth transition for staff when handling and issuing detentions.

 I hope you understand my idea and if you have any further queries regarding this, please feel free to contact me.
journeys: Logging Behaviour and Rewards|Parent and guardian messages and notes|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Logging Behaviour and Setting Detentions|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

comment:
Hello,

 Our current behaviour set up at Goffs Churchgate academy works around an escalation system. Due to the nature of incident workflows and custom workflows overriding these, our behaviour policy does not work correctly in Arbor. A way to improve this could be to put both workflows on the same level so they can co-exist in a behaviour setup. This would enable other schools, not just ours, to have an escalating behaviour policy with behaviour sanctions sitting on different levels (Level 2 Negative and Level 3 Negative for example). Due to the current behaviour setup this is not possible after my discussions with Michelle yesterday. I believe that this will enable custom workflows and incident workflows to co-exist in the same setup both holding equal value as some behaviour incidents on one or different levels may require different actions, but when combining them they offer the same sanction.

 I hope you are able to understand the way I have worded this and if you have any further queries, please feel free to contact me.

summary: Restricting the setting of future detentions so that a student does not end up with multiple detentions
comment:
As a school, we intend to use detentions as opportunities for reflection, allowing students and staff to engage in restorative conversations. This means that when a student already has a scheduled detention, there is no need for another one to be set, as the necessary conversation can take place during that time.

 However, we have found that some students end up with detentions scheduled up to three weeks in advance.

 Is there a way to restrict the setting of detentions so that students do not receive multiple future detentions?

 Currently, the only solution I can think of is to open detention slots one at a time on a daily basis, but this would be rather onerous and we do not have the capacity to do this.

 With our previous MIS, we were able to restrict this so that a student who has been set a detention for the next available slot would not be set a future one. These undermine behaviour systems and I believe most schools benefit from the ability to do so.

summary: Attendance Data On Arbor MAT site
comment:
Hello,

 There are a number of points I would like to raise around attendance on the Arbor MAT site. Currently, the whole academic year attendance percentages for secondary schools are inaccurate, as they include year 11 sessions after the summer term half term, which the DfE exclude from their attendance data, so could this be looked at please to bring in line with the way DfE calculate attendance?

 Also, I would be so happy if the Arbor attendance data could somehow match the DfE figures. For example, I think the DfE data only includes students who have been on roll for a certain number of sessions, while i think Arbor includes everyone. It would just be so nice to reconcile figures, like when I look at the DfE statistical publication and it shows a % attendance figure for the 23/24 academic year, it would be so nice to run a report in Arbor and see the same figure. I know this is a lot to do and get right, but wouldn't it be amazing if we could achieve this and see the same figures. I also know that staff can probably make retrospective historical amendments to Arbor, which is why the stats may not reconcile, but it would be amazing if they could. Or, would it be possible to build into Arbor stats from past DfE publications, this would make dashboard creation even easier, as currently I go away and pull out the historical stats from Analyse school performance or the DfE statistical releases. Having these available on Arbor would make things easier.

 In the MAT Arbor MIS, when I navigate to Analytics -> Attendance and then click on the box for statutory attendance a graph appears showing attendance for this academic year by phase. However, this compares the attendance up to today for this year with the attendance in the whole of last academic year. This is good, but of course the secondary data for last year is inaccurate, as it includes the year 11 data after the summer half term, but what I would really like to see is an toggle option for comparing 1. the attendance % for this academic year up to this point with the corresponding period last year, or 2. for the whole of last year.

 On the MAT MIS site and also school sites, attendance breakdowns by characteristics are shown, so we have No SEN attendance vs SEN attendance. However, what would be really helpful is to also break this down further by SEN provision, so attendance by SEN K code vs attendance by SEN E code. This additional breakdown is available for suspensions on the MAT site, but not for attendance currently.

 One of the things I would find so helpful is if the attendance sessions without a mark on Arbor were flagged up somewhere in a report. For example, if I build a custom report I can ask for specific attendance codes like /, \, N, M, S etc, but I can't return any sessions which are missing codes. So these are the sessions which don't have a mark. Also, I can only return the attendance codes from the custom reports which are currently used by the DfE, but it may well be that some academies may have used old codes like the old holiday agreed code or a Covid reason for absence. I think the system still allows for entry of these old codes unless I am mistaken, so I need a way of checking to see if academies have used these, along for easily checking on sessions with missing codes. I know I could use Power BI, but I would find it so helpful if this were built into an Arbor custom report.

 In the school Arbor sites, the Students -> Attendance -> Statistics is a brilliant feature. However, in the wonderful filter menu, I don't believe there is an option to view attendance by day of the week. For example, to see Friday attendance quick for the year quickly in here. This would be a helpful addition.

 Thank you,

 Ian
journeys: Accessing Vital Pupil Context|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Accessing Vital Pupil Context and SEND Passports

comment:
Safeguarding

 18 months in, we still have serious concerns over the lack of safeguarding features in Arbor. I have bought this up several times before, in the road map, and in meetings i have had with customer success managers but still no further forward.

 We need a quick and easy way for our staff to identify at risk or vulnerable students. They also need to report immediately if a vulnerable student is not in their lesson.

 Flags on the lesson dashboard are simply not good enough, there are so many reasons that students may have a flag, teachers do not have the time to go through every one.

 We have started adding a red ring around students photos for those that we consider vulnerable.

 This is obviously takes time and is a lot of work for admin staff.

 It would be helpful if Arbor could adopt this feature or add a way to identify these students from the register page.

 This isn't just urgent, it is critical.

summary: Allow us to report on Medical Notes
comment:
We noticed that pastoral managers were adding medical conditions and dietary requirements as pinned pastoral notes. I looked into this by reporting on all pastoral notes and manually highlighting the ones i intended to change to medical. some were easy and very obviously medical conditions eg. asthma, epilepsy, allergies, etc so i created these as medical conditions and/or dietary requirements instead. however there were things that arent quite named defined medical conditions but i would describe more as a medical note than a pastoral note - e.g. legs hurt after exercise, can get headaches after screen use, can be prone to fainting. etc. so i went through and added these as medical notes and deleted the pastoral note. however, i then realised you *cannot report on medical notes*. why is this? this means that if a teacher is taking children on a trip and wants to pull all medical info, they would miss important medical information like this if they are a medical note not medical condition. so for example the girl we have that is prone to fainting, she hasn't got a named defined medical condition for this so therefore this shouldn't be flagged as a condition but should still be recorded under medical and would *definitely* be relevant when taking children on trips.

 the current workaround would be to either: artificially inflate the number of medical conditions in school by recording everything medical or health related as a 'Medical _condition ' which I don't think is accurate or right. Other than this, I could record them as pastoral notes and write the word 'medical' as the summary to allow this to be reportable/filterable but again this seems incorrect and strange as it's not a pastoral issue it's a medical one.

 the overall reason that this should be added is related to safeguarding; it's critical that staff can easily access medical information for a cohort whenever they need to so im worried that currently important medical info could be missed when reporting on this. if we could add an additional field when reporting ABOUT STUDENTS, so you could do Student Name, DOB, class, medical conditions, medical NOTES, contact details.

 this would mean anything relevant and medical related could all be seen in one place.

 Thank you for reading, i sincerely hope this can be actioned.

summary: Conceal reasons for absence in planned absence on class register screen
comment:
I've asked if a note in planned absence could be concealed and not displayed on screen when teachers are taking a register. This has proved problematic. This is the response for Ask Arbor.

 Mrs Trotter, your idea of having planned absence notes that remain linked to attendance records and certificates but are concealed by default on the class register with just a discreet indicator sounds very practical. Unfortunately, Arbor MIS does not currently have a built-in feature specifically for marking planned absence notes as concealed or showing a small flag instead of the full note on the register screen.

 The current system will display planned absence notes on the register to staff for clarity when taking attendance. However, some schools handle this by training staff to glance for absence flags or working with Arbor support on custom report options or register views.

 I recommend submitting this as a feature request to Arbor's development team to consider for future updates. Meanwhile, I could assist in checking if there are any less visible ways to record or show planned absence notes or configuring reports or alerts differently for your needs.

 Would you like me to search for current best practises or possible workarounds within Arbor for this?

comment:
It would be really really helpful for the reports we would like to build if behaviour incidents could have subcategories. For example the overall behaviour could be 'Discrimination' and then within that, staff pick from a drop-down of the type of discrimination. This allows for more consistent reporting as it without this you would need to have 8+ different behavioural incident types for all the types of discriminations.

 We would also like to use behaviour incidents to track when students are trained on different Tech equipment. This should work well as behaviour incidents are reportable, tied to a date and staff member, so would provide the schools with exactly what they want (they want to know when a student has been trained on a particular type of equipment and with which staff member). However, the downside of this is that currently we will have to add one overarching behaviour incident type, 'DT Equipment Training' for example, then rely on teachers filling in the narrative with the equipment name e.g. drill, hand saw, etc. However, if we want to use a live feed to report on this in excel for example, or even within Arbor, filtering etc will be a nightmare as the field is free text so teachers will all write these things differently. We don't want to have 20+ behaviour types in the list that are just DT tools as this will completely clog the behaviour field. Therefore it would be great to have sub-categories, so we could allow teachers to pick DT Equipment Training and then use the pre-defined drop-down fields to select the specific tool, NOT opening this up to free text.

summary: Notification for all logged communications (telephone calls, pastoral notes, parent/carer conversations)
comment:
When staff log a telephone call, pastoral note, or other communication in Arbor, there is no automatic notification to relevant staff (e.g. class teachers, pastoral staff, or SLT). This means that important conversations between the office and parents/carers/students are not always being picked up promptly. At present, the only way to check these entries is via the school mail inbox, which is inefficient and risks information being missed. To work around this, we are currently having to screenshot every entry and email it manually to the relevant staff.

 It would be helpful if you could introduce a notification system that alerts relevant staff when a communication is logged (with options to filter or assign which staff receive which notifications). This will ensure that important conversations are not overlooked, improves safeguarding and communication between staff and will reduce duplication of work and reliance on screenshots/emails.

summary: Automated Behaviour alert email to form tutors
comment:
What I would like to achieve:

 When a student reaches a set behaviour threshold (e.g. X behaviour incidents or X behaviour points within a defined period), I would like Arbor to automatically:

 Escalate the next incident to a higher behaviour level (e.g. from Level -1 to Level -2).

 Automatically assign that incident to the student‚Äôs Form Tutor,

 Trigger an email notification to the Form Tutor as soon as the incident is assigned.

 This would replicate the workflow previously used in PARS/SIMS, where form tutors received an instant email when a pupil crossed a behaviour threshold, allowing early intervention and consistent oversight.

 Questions / support needed:

 Can Arbor support automatic escalation based on a threshold (‚Äúnumber of incidents in X time‚Äù or ‚Äútotal behaviour points accumulated‚Äù)?

 If so, can you confirm the best way to configure this under Students > Behaviour > Setup > Incidents > Automatic Incident Workflows?

 Which permissions do I need to access and edit these workflows? (I believe this may require Behaviour: Behaviour Policy or School: Action: Behaviour: Administer, but please confirm.)

 Once escalated, can Arbor automatically assign the incident to Form Tutors and send them an immediate notification email?

 If there are any package requirements (e.g., this needing the Perform or Comms package), please let me know.

 What the final workflow should achieve:

 Teacher logs an incident

 Arbor recalculates the pupil‚Äôs behaviour total

 If the threshold is met, the incident escalates

 Form Tutor automatically receives an email with the incident link

 No manual action required on staff side

 If this is already possible in our current setup, I‚Äôd really appreciate a short set of instructions or for someone to enable the required permissions so I can configure it.

 Thank you very much for your help ‚Äî I really appreciate your time.

 Set up Student Portal.

comment:
HI,

 we should be able to view the data in charts, nicely presented, with the ability to apply timeline, export the data with additional columns that we would like to add... export the data to wherever... Custom Group, Power BI as a live feed... more user friendly drill into the details at year group, registration form, student, demographics level.... Charts, pls! Charts that tell the story....eg. average learning output... average grade across key stages... is it coming down or up.... not just attainment % this year vs last year, but by month, by term, by demographics... eg. where our SEN or FSM or EAL students accelerate? What are they good at? where do they struggle? IS their main struggle the persistent absence or low attendance or certain behaviour, eg. distracting lessons? What is the % of such students that contribute to lesson distraction? how do we compare to statistical UK averages? Every dashboard should be named after purpose of the story (or we should have the ability to re-name them) and build them easily = visual presentation is key. Maybe it would make sense for you to send a survey across your clients that capture what stories / analytics schools capture.

 Last but not least, you've got to seriously improve KS4 and make KS5 analytics available. Currently, it's at 'infant' stage. Our trust is looking for EBACC 5+ (accumulative percentage of grades, all analysed by Demographics, P8, A8 - also, accummulative grades, averages, we also track students who made the most progress (not necessary they are on or above or below target, they simply make the highest progress or regress at whatever stage they are... it's difficult to track those currently. Also, we have current grade per subject, we have OCTA = On course To Attain = that's what teachers think they will be achieving and actual grade. Currently actual = exam results are in sort of different hemisphere - difficult for teachers to have a clear picture how their evaluation went, what is the residual (margin of error)... I was exploring the possiblity to add actual (eg. GCSE results) onto our Post 16 summative assessments, but I couldn't find a way to do this.

 Your team is super cool and has brilliant minds, surely there is a lot to improve, however, my tip to your team - act as software architects /engineers, but think at a typical teacher level, step into their shoes and don't step out - this is what leads your product to become a leader in the market. By the way, I work for two trusts with over 30 schools and I'm thinking 'selling' Arbor to one trust that still operates on Bromcom. But I've got to see your Assessments 2.0 in operation! It's key! Currently, I'm not enjoying operating it - it's a bit like Cybertruck - too many sharp angles. Ah, other feedback from the teaching staff....so, when I release the data collection policy, when teachers click on the link from TO DO on the homepage, they are not able to see targets. I get your logic why targets are not shown by default, however, for teachers the targets are key, even if targets should not be an influence when they enter the grades for the term. Or another feedback from other topic which is painful - it should be made easier to manage, off set unopened or incomplete registers. I know there are recommendations how to do this, but not all lessons that don't happen have a support staff cover, and I know we have the functionality to suspend the lessons, but the reality in many schools is different - we just need a better tool to cancel incomplete registers alerts on staff to do list, because they become a 'noise' and staff miss the important stuff on to do list. In other words, more elasticity, more control to schools.

 Sorry, my thoughts weren't were structure, but at least I said what was on my mind.

comment:
We recently noticed that lots of our students medical conditions were stored in Medical Notes, rather than Conditions, meaning that they couldn't be pinned to registers or added to custom reports. It also meant that students with medical conditions weren't being marked as 'has medical condition' on their profiles.

 This seems to be from a combination of human error and possibly also CTFs writing to the wrong place (or it could have been during our migration from SIMS). I called Arbor support during half term and was told that medical notes could not be exported.

 It took four members of staff 1.5 working days to go through every student in the school and add medical conditions based on the medical notes. Being able to export medical notes would have sped this process up considerably, as we could have filtered out the students with no medical notes. Being able to add medical notes to custom reports would also add context/depth to reporting if further information is ever required.

comment:
One of the great things about using Integris for the register as an admin (I am not talking as a teacher) was that I was able to quickly have a WEEKLY view at who was in class that week (with repeat absences being obvious) and who was not and I could see all the codes used for the week and for particular children (with a quick access to the notes to see why they had been absent). From the same weekly register I was able to see all the "N" and "O" code and act quickly with parents to find out why their children were absent. From there, I could get a quick list which would help notify the kitchen of the absentees via ParentPay.

 I could also have a look at the entire register for each class for any previous/forward week that I was looking for just by changing the week we were on. It was really quick and easy to use. Now it is much more cumbersome and slow. For each day I have to go to Student/absentees/absentees by date and amend the "N" or "O" that I found before being able to use this on ParentPay.

 To summarise, I very much like the weekly view past/forward as it was easy to modify the register for each class and find patterns of absences and the reasons of those absences.

 I hope it makes sense but if not, please do contact me directly

summary: It should be possible to print exam seating plans using preferred names, not just legal names
comment:
We have problems with examination seating plans only being printable using the legal name due to the sensitivities around gender. We have students who are transitioning and also students who associate as the other gender. The problems this causes are;

 1. Seating plans are put up in a public space so students can find their seat before entering the hall. Using the legal name means that students are able to see the legal name of peers, which in some cases makes it apparent that their birth sex is different.

 2. Invigilators are external members of staff who will not know any details about these students. While we can make them aware, there is no specific need for them to know in the role they fulfill and there is too much risk that the "wrong" (ie legal) name may be accidentally used.

 As a consequence of the above we have to leave the affected students off the seating plan to avoid the potential to reveal highly personal information. This creates it's own issues.

 If the preferred name could be used instead it would avoid any of the above.

summary: Being able to produce/download a report on items that are pinned
comment:
In our school the only thing that should be pinned on students profiles are if they are is ‚ÄúPolaris Pursuit‚Äù student. If a student is ‚ÄúPolaris Pursuit‚Äù this pastoral note is pinned, so they are flagged on the registers to enable staff to give specific virtue points on Arbor. Every other note that is created should not be pinned.

 After emailing schools ICT they have confirmed that not all pinned items appear as a flag on staff registers, only pastoral notes, behaviour notes, SEN notes and safeguarding notes. Medical conditions and dietary requirements do not appear as a flag on staff registers.

summary: Use retrospective dates for internal isolations
comment:
I need to use past dates when assigning internal isolations but this has changed so that I can only select today onwards. Is there a settings change I can make?

 See notes from my training with SBS:

 Just to update you on the internal exclusion dates that we discussed previously and I had raised with Arbor. They have confirmed as we thought that when logging/amending the exclusions you can't from this area set students for past dates. Arbor have provided their reasoning for this which will detail below.

 They have however explained there is a workaround you can use which I will also detail below. They have suggested in the meantime if you would prefer to leave feedback with Arbor about making a change to the system you can do so using the feedback options on Arbor.

 If you have any questions then please let me know.

 It is expected for Arbor to work like this; it just avoids the teachers and other staff having to scroll through load and loads of sessions the later it gets in the year. summary: Enable Direct Communication Between Parents/Carers and Staff via the Arbor Parent App
comment:
*I am submitting a number of feedback requests and would greatly appreciate being contacted or having a virtual meeting arranged to discuss them further. I would also be very happy to support any beta testing to help make this service even more efficient and sector-leading.

 *

 Currently, the Arbor Parent App does not allow parents and carers to communicate directly with individual teachers or relevant staff. The only option is the general in-app message, which redirects to the main school office inbox. This system is unsustainable, particularly for larger schools, as it creates unnecessary workload and delays in messages reaching the appropriate person.

 While parents can reply directly to staff if they receive an email sent via Arbor, this does not resolve the issue ‚Äî most families primarily use the Arbor App, and there is no in-app function to message staff directly.

 Key Benefits and Safeguarding Considerations:

 * Improved communication flow: Enables parents and carers to contact the relevant teacher, tutor, or pastoral lead directly through the app, ensuring messages reach the correct person quickly.

 * Reduced administrative workload: The main office would no longer need to triage and manually forward large volumes of messages.

 * Supports engagement and partnership: Strengthens school‚Äìhome collaboration through clear, direct, and traceable communication.

 * Safeguarding control: Each school should have full control over this feature including the ability to turn it on or off, set who can be contacted, and define whether replies are allowed.

 * Customisable permissions: Schools could choose to limit communication to specific staff groups (e.g. class teachers, Heads of Year, pastoral staff) in line with safeguarding and communication policies. However, schools must have the ability to toggle this feature on or off, restrict it etc.

 * Student App extension (optional): Similar functionality could be developed for the Student App, allowing teacher‚Äìstudent communication for homework or feedback. However, schools must have the ability to toggle this feature on or off, restrict it to teacher‚Äìstudent only, and disable student-to-student messaging entirely for safeguarding compliance.

 * Feature parity with previous systems: EduLink successfully offered this functionality, demonstrating it can be implemented securely and efficiently.

 Providing this flexibility, where schools control activation and permissions, would make Arbor far more functional, sustainable, and safeguarding-compliant, while significantly improving the quality of school‚Äìhome communication.
journeys: Accessing Vital Pupil Context|Parent and guardian messages and notes|Setting and Reviewing Assignments (Homework)|Logging Behaviour and Setting Detentions|Setting and Reviewing Homework (Assignments)|Accessing Vital Pupil Context and SEND Passports|Requesting Immediate Support (On-Call / Emergency Alert)|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

summary: Develop a Dedicated Arbor Teacher App
comment:
*I am submitting a number of feedback requests and would greatly appreciate being contacted or having a virtual meeting arranged to discuss them further. I would also be very happy to support any beta testing to help make this service even more efficient and sector-leading.*

 Currently, Arbor does not have a dedicated Teacher App, whereas previous systems such as ClassCharts and EduLink offered this as a core feature. A teacher-specific app would provide essential functionality and accessibility, particularly for quick, on-the-move use during lessons, duties, and pastoral interactions.

 Key Benefits and Potential Features:

 * Quick behaviour logging: Instant access to record positive or negative points, detentions, and notes during lessons or around the site.

 * Register and attendance: Ability to take or edit registers directly from a mobile device without relying on a desktop or laptop.

 * Homework management: View, set, and mark homework on the go, with push notifications for submissions or student queries.

 * Student information access: Secure, real-time access to key details such as timetables, SEN info, safeguarding notes (where appropriate), and contact logs.

 * Pastoral and safeguarding alerts: Receive relevant notifications for key pastoral updates or incidents logged for students they teach or tutor.

 * Calendar and cover integration: View daily schedules, room changes, and cover details quickly and clearly.

 * Ease of use: A simplified, intuitive interface designed for mobile and tablet use, reducing admin time and increasing engagement.

 * Improved mobile experience: Current functionality on mobile devices via the Arbor website is poor and clunky, making quick tasks difficult and time-consuming.

 A teacher app is now a standard expectation in school systems, not an optional extra, and would significantly improve Arbor‚Äôs usability, accessibility, and efficiency for staff.

summary: Allow parents/carers to upload evidence (e.g. medical notes or appointment letters) directly through the Arbor App when reporting absences to improve accuracy, efficiency, and safeguarding compliance.
comment:
*I am submitting a number of feedback requests and would greatly appreciate being contacted or having a virtual meeting arranged to discuss them further. I would also be very happy to support any beta testing to help make this service even more efficient and sector-leading.*

 Currently, parents and carers cannot attach evidence (e.g. medical notes, appointment letters, travel documents) when reporting an absence, which leads to fragmented communication, extra admin work, and delays in verifying absences. It would be extremely useful for Parents and Carers to have the option to upload supporting evidence (e.g. appointment letters, medical notes, travel documents) directly through the Arbor Parent App when reporting a student‚Äôs absence. This would streamline communication and strengthen record keeping for schools.

 Key Benefits:

 * Improved accuracy and efficiency: Evidence is submitted at the same time as the absence, reducing the need for follow-up emails or calls.

 * Better record management: Documents are securely stored and linked to the student‚Äôs attendance record for audit and safeguarding purposes.

 * Reduced admin workload: Staff no longer need to manually upload or chase evidence from parents.

 * Enhanced parent experience: Quick, secure and user-friendly way for families to share required information.

 * Supports safeguarding and compliance: Ensures transparent, verifiable records for authorised absences and local authority reporting.

summary: Allow club sessions to be ended the same way as timetable slots when changes are needed
comment:
We sometimes start a club but for various reasons it doesn't continue for as long as expected. When that happens, it is really hard to end it (and thus remove it from timetables) without just deleting it as if it never existed - this is the instruction "To change this, you would need to delete their memberships, amend the timeslots, and then add their memberships again. They will only be added to the sessions that exist based on the new timeslot dates.".

 I'm not sure if this is a requirement for paid clubs, but it seems really onerous for free clubs, particularly when standard lesson timetable slots can be ended with ease when needed, without having to remove students first.

 If it was possible for club slots to work the same way so that we could change the end date without losing the history of attendees and their attendance it would be a much more straightforward (and less laborious) task.

 Lastly, the error message that is currently displayed when you do try to end the sessions is quite baffling;

 "{color:#205081}It isn't possible to change the effective start and/or end date for this timetable slot._* Some sessions that fall behind the new interval and should be deleted. However they already have students registered. *_Please cancel the club registration from these sessions before changing the timetable slot effective dates{color}"

comment:
i have submitted this previously as a development but its not acknowledged, i don't know who to inform.

 The homework settings for our Trust (100's teachers surveyed) does not work well.

 If a teacher sets a homework that does not require submitting in arbor, the teacher must still waste time performing admin tasks outlining it is submitted from the student/parent portal. This creates workload.

 For example, If a teacher asks a pupil to draw a picture, the teacher then has to physically see the picture, and then go into arbor, and find the class, find the homework, and mark all as submitted. This step, for all the goodwill of teachers is not happening in our Trust, and I suspect nationwide. The homework stays in the student portal and parent portal as unsubmitted and causes us to field complaints from parents that our system is outdated.

 This unnecessary teacher admin step could be avoided, if there was a check box beside the homework each individual student could 'tick' which then marks the homework as submitted, removing it from the portals. We had this feature on our previous homework product INSIGHT, a far cheaper product.

 Please consider improving this utility and reduce our complaints.

comment:
I want to be able to download student timetables to show the lesson info for a day (but ideally also have the option to extend this to show the information relating to their lessons in the whole timetable cycle if required) into Excel with all lesson info (class, subject, location and staff as a minimum).

 This is an issue I have had several times over the last year when we need to plan for some but not all students to come out of lessons. My current example is the flu vaccinations in school this Wednesday, where the whole school has been offered the vaccine but only around half are having it. This isn't as simple as scheduling a time for a full class of students as the uptake is different from each class each lesson as the classes for most years different each lesson. I also need to avoid some subjects such as PE and Tech where removing a large number of the students is difficult mid-activity.

 If I want this list I currently have the option of downloading from Timetable>Student Timetable. This contains the information I want but is a pdf and not in an easily manipulated format i.e. just rows of data like a xlsx or csv.

 The other option I can see is to create a custom report which I can export to Excel. I have created a report on students which lists their name and the class name and location of each lesson on a given date. However, there is no option here to include the lesson teacher so I now have to creta another report with this info and cross reference it. Reusing this custom report is also long winded as have to change the date individually for each "event" and "location" column in the report.

summary: Printing larger exam room seating plans - make them legible
comment:
When printing exam seating plans for larger rooms, such as Halls or Sports Halls, where the majority of exams would be held for students (e.g. 11 rows with 21 columns), can you print using the whole of the sheet available?

 At present, when we go to print our Sports Hall (SPH) seating plan, the full page is not utilised properly, even when we do not select 'fit to page'.

 Can you please set up a room of this size, sit some candidates variously around the room, and print this page to A3 and see what you see. In our experience, the text is small, the seating plan is off to 1 side and is very small considering the size of the page. At present, the print space used is approximately 2/3 of the width and length of the page available.

 For our invigilators, who are mostly retired and so do not always have the 20/20 vision, and our candidates, who are students between 15-18 yrs old, this is unreadable.

 We print to A3 to make it readable for both students and invigilators and currently this is not the case. We have found a work around, which has nothing to do with Arbor, because the seating plans for our SPH print so poorly from Arbor. This is a work around our exams officer was given, by an exams officer from another school, because it is a known issue amongst exam officers that Arbor prints larger room seating plans so poorly, even when blown up to A3.

 Can you format the output better, so the seating plans fill the whole page so the text can be bigger?

 If there is already a solution to this, can you provide it on the Arbor help center (https://support.arbor-education.com/hc/en-us) or on Arbor HQ? The community posts (from users) talk about making it 'easier to read' but 'agree it's still very small' when unticking the 'fit to page' option, but this is not a solution, just better than keeping 'fit to page' selected. If this is the solution, why not have 'fit to page' unticked as standard?

 Look to forward to any kind of response to my suggestions.
journeys: Managing Classroom Seating Plans|Taking Period-by-Period Registers|Designing and Managing Seating Plans

comment:
From: David <doneil@bishopdouglass.barnet.sch.uk>

 Thank you for your reply

 Can you see the merit in custom interventions updating with up-to-date class lists?

 Let‚Äôs say student A is in maths set A, but is struggling with their maths quite seriously

 Now student A is moved to maths set C,

 However, for ongoing intervention of higher maths (set A),

 student A now is in the wrong set and needs to be manually adjusted.

 instead of importing direct students, it would be good to be able to import the class, allowing for better administration

 David

 From: Ryan (Arbor Education) <myteam@arbor-education.com>

 Sent: 15 December 2025 11:10

 To: David O'Neil <doneil@bishopdouglass.barnet.sch.uk>

 Subject: Custom interventions

 Hi David,

 I'm really sorry for the delay on resolution for this ticket. I understand this would have impact on your school so wanted to explore all possible avenues before providing a final outcome.

 Unfortunately, it is only possible for intervention attendees to derive from an enrolment into that intervention. They cannot be pulled from elsewhere and must be enrolled in that intervention.

 Ad hoc intervention sessions are intended to be used on a one-off basis, and so it would not be possible to create them from a class list automatically. You can create ad hoc intervention sessions from the Lesson Dashboard, but this would need to be done manually each time. This is because standard interventions pull the students who are enrolled into them, and can do so continuously. Considering this, ad hoc interventions, as one-offs, allow for more flexibility on when/where a student is added to an intervention, but have no automation.

 I appreciate that this is not the outcome you were hoping for, and I'm sorry that I could not provide a more preferable resolution.

 If you have any further questions, please don't hesitate to let me know and I'll be happy to help.

 All the best,

 Ryan

comment:
I‚Äôm getting in touch to share an idea that could significantly enhance the way schools record first aid and medication incidents through Arbor.

 There is currently a third-party solution called First Aid Forms that integrates with Arbor, and it has highlighted how valuable a simple, staff-friendly interface can be. It works by pulling pupil data from Arbor and providing an intuitive tablet/iPad app that allows staff to:

 * Select the child quickly from Arbor-linked data

 * Choose whether they are recording First Aid or Administering Medication

 * Log symptoms, add a description of the incident and upload photos if needed

 * Select the incident location (with areas customisable to each school)

 * Record treatment or medication given

 * Use a speech-to-text feature so staff can dictate the written sections, supporting colleagues who may find writing difficult and ensuring clear, accurate, grammatical communication

 * Automatically send a notification to parents with the description, treatment given, and any photos

 The app also offers reporting capabilities, allowing schools to analyse trends such as:

 ‚Ä¢ areas with the highest number of incidents

 ‚Ä¢ common injury types

 ‚Ä¢ time-of-day patterns, etc.

 A key benefit is that the whole process typically takes under one minute, unless it‚Äôs a serious incident requiring more detail. This speed is crucial for staff who are supervising children and don‚Äôt have time for lengthy logging systems.

 Another important feature is that it can work offline, allowing staff to record an incident even without Wi-Fi. Once the device reconnects, it syncs the information and sends the parent notification automatically. This would be really beneficial to our school and I'm sure many more that use Arbor to have a quick method of completing first aid logs digitally and notifying parents.
journeys: Accessing Vital Pupil Context|Parent and guardian messages and notes|Logging Behaviour and Setting Detentions|Accessing Vital Pupil Context and SEND Passports|Requesting Immediate Support (On-Call / Emergency Alert)|Class and subject communication (students & guardians)

role: Urgent for ESA school: Time out button
comment:
As the SENCO at ESA, we have a number of neurodivergent students who require a 'time out' of lessons in order to help them to regulate their overwhelm and distractions.

 What we would really like to do is, from a teaching user perspective, be able to press a button which signfiys the start of a time out for that student who has a time out card and then by hitting the button again when the student returns from their time out as well. It would then be able to be time stamped by date, period/lesson and the number of minutes on the timeout. Teachers will only need to push the button.

 Then from my point of view as SENCO, I can run a report which gives me information like when they were issued the card, how often they use their cards, what lessons, and how many minutes at a time. This way we can track the use of them, make sure that students are using them correctly, review them to see if they're still needed and find potential trigger points in a day where students may need further support.

 I think this would really help our students and staff and would be looking to implement this ASAP.

 Happy to discuss / call with further information if needed.

 Thank you

comment:
I am writing to highlight some challenges we are currently experiencing with the absence recording process, which we believe could benefit from improvement.

 When a child is absent for the entire day, it is important that the procedure for recording this is as quick and efficient as possible. However, after receiving notification of a same-day absence from a parent, we are unable to record the absence for both AM and PM sessions in one step. At present, this requires opening two separate registers, which is proving to be time-consuming.

 Additionally, we have identified an issue with the planned absence feature. Changes or edits made within this function are not visible to teachers, likely because the data does not transfer across to their system. This has led to teachers unintentionally overriding critical absence information, creating significant complications.

 We would appreciate any guidance or solutions you can offer to help streamline this process and prevent these issues from recurring.

summary: Better restrict club leaders ability to view unnecessary information
comment:
Hi

 A lot of clubs in schools are run by external staff that are not employees of the school. We'd like for them to be able to take club registers without allowing them access to other areas within Arbor. We currently have to take registers either on paper or spreadsheet and then manually transpose that information to Arbor. This results in additional work for the admin staff and also has the risk of human error. If we try and allow an external club leader to take the club register, not only can they view *all* clubs, not just the one that they are associated with, but they can also view all trip information. The ability for someone with the permission "Extra Curricular: View" to be able to view all clubs is not the end of the world, but this permission needs to be developed to be more granular so that staff can be granted only appropriate permissions, e.g "Extra Curricular (Trips): View", "Extra Curricular (Clubs): View" or "Extra Curricular (All): View", etc.

 Thank you

 Stuart

comment:
I discovered an issue recently with formulas in duplicated custom reports not duplicating correctly.

 I flagged this with the help team. The second line support got back to me to say this is expected behaviour.....

 _"When you duplicate a custom report, the system creates new report columns which get a new column ID each time. This is why the formula cannot map to the previously mapped columns, as those column IDs no longer exist on the duplicated report. Thus, it picks the next best thing. You will need to re-map the columns for each of the formulas on the duplicated report."_

 I find it extremely frustrating that none of the help centre guidance explains this, or there is no warning on the screen when duplicating reports, or the formula column doesn't show an error.

 I have been creating formulas and duplicating custom reports for over a year now assuming that everything was working as it should. My data has been incorrect this whole time, I am literally fuming.

 I am now have the arduous task of going through all my reports to check the formulas.

 I would suggest that you add big warning signs about this to help future users.

comment:
Hi,

 We currently use Interventions for students being sent anywhere that isn't their regular classroom, reasons vary, it could be due to their support needs such as anxiety or it could be due to an incident that has taken place in school to name a few. The issue we have is that we have no way of pulling an attendance report for these interventions.

 When this happens, students are allocated their regular register and one for their intervention, they will receive an absent code for their regular lesson, and a present mark for their intervention however, when we pull reports, it always just shows us their attendance for their regular lesson - which will be absent. This triggers us identifying the students are missing, when they aren't and messages go home unneccessarily.

 It would be very helpful to be able to include attendance reports that include intervention attendance so we have a full picture of actual attendance in a seamless way.
